title: 3M Company (MMM) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of 3M Company (MMM) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A modest 4% growth rate reflects the mature nature of 3M's end markets and the drag of ongoing legal settlement payouts on free cash flow generation.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate accounts for the heightened risk profile due to historical litigation overhangs, balanced by the stable, cash-generative nature of its core operations.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate assumes 3M will track slightly below long-term GDP growth, acknowledging the structural challenges in its legacy business mix.

thesisParagraphs: 3M is a classic turnaround story burdened by massive historical liabilities. The core business—producing essential materials and industrial goods—remains highly cash-generative with robust gross margins. However, the multi-billion dollar settlements for PFAS and combat earplugs have fundamentally altered the company's risk profile and capital allocation priorities.

Our valuation model attempts to look past the immediate cash drain of these settlements to the underlying earnings power of the enterprise. While the spin-off of the healthcare business (Solventum) removes a key growth engine, it allows management to streamline operations. The current valuation suggests the market has largely priced in the worst of the legal news, but a significant margin of safety is still lacking.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: How do the legal settlements affect 3M's valuation?
faq.answer: The settlements represent massive cash outflows over the coming decade. Our DCF model adjusts expected free cash flow downward to account for these structured payments, which acts as a significant drag on the final intrinsic value.

faq.question: Why use a relatively high discount rate for a blue-chip company?
faq.answer: While 3M is historically a blue-chip, the uncertainty surrounding the long-term environmental remediation costs of PFAS introduces significant risk, justifying an 8.5% discount rate to demand a higher return for that risk.

faq.question: Is the Solventum spin-off factored into this model?
faq.answer: Yes, this model reflects the 'RemainCo' 3M, excluding the cash flows from the spun-off healthcare segment, resulting in a lower overall revenue base but a potentially more focused operational profile.
